metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_1062
metadata.comment:
Rationale: In this study authors generated antisera against calcineurin A alpha by immunizing a rabbit and used it to detect calcineurin A alpha in different subcellular fractions prepared from rat brain. Though data showing the relative amounts of calcineurin A alpha in the different fractions is only presented in a table format and blots are not shown, authors claim that Calcineurin A alpha is enriched in the P2C fraction containing synaptosomes, and when this fraction was lysed, 80% of the protein was recovered in the soluble fraction, which suggest that calcineurin A alpha is most abundant in the synaptic cytosol.. Experimental description: "For quantitative determination of the isoforms in tissue
homogenates from various regions of the rat brain and in aliquots of subcellular fractions, male Wistar rats (weighing 150-200 g) were decapitated, and brains were quickly removed."

The antisera was prepared against the rat Calcineurin A alpha sequence as described in Ito et al., 1989.
